channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Heave Ho 2 Preview – Hold my hand and help me make a chicken burger
item.content:encoded:
There’s an unbridled chaos and joy to playing Heave Ho 2, a raucous delight to teaming up and struggling to overcome its physics-based challenges. It is very much like the original… but more.

At its core, Heave Ho 2 is a very simple game. You’re a head with a pair of long, gangly arms, and you’re very good at grabbing onto things. Using just one analogue stick and left and right triggers of a gamepad, you can grab onto things, swing around, and fling yourself between ledges. Except sometimes these ledges are really far away, so you need to create long-limbed chains and go swinging with friends or strangers – not like that. Definitely not like that, when you can now unleash an explosive fart and violently expel people away from you.

That’s where it gets chaotic, especially with strangers (from a co-op gaming perspective) trying to figure out the physics, communicate who should grab with what and when, and the odd little bit of mischief thrown in for good measure. It’s very easy to have grabbed both another hand and a surface at the same time – or at least be so fearful of falling to your death that you won’t let go – and tricky in general to coordinate. Even when you know what you want to do, you might just have a moment of brain fade, letting go with the wrong hand at the wrong time and send you and/or your co-op buddies flying off screen, to be turned into a colourful spurt of yoghurt.

So far, so Heave Ho. You build chains of people, swing through levels and try to get all of your to the end together. Where Heave Ho 2 looks to really take things beyond the original is with more interactive environments and themes.

In a kitchen themed level, it was a touch of Overcooked as we had to meet the order of a grumpy looking chef to clamber around ropes, open containers, grab and layer the constituent parts of a chicken burger (including a timely squirt of mustard), before dinging a bell to have judgement passed. Dropping into a gallery room, it eventually became clear that we had to place three artefacts onto plinths and have them in the right order – cue awkwardly trying to drag one bust over another and not tip the one that was correct off its spot. And then there was the surprisingly serene and calm space level, swinging into a rocket and then blasting off using four thrusters to help direct us between asteroids. We basically each took charge of one thruster, and it went about as smoothly as Artemis II did.

More varied gameplay isn’t the only thing coming to this sequel, and while I can absolutely say that this will be best played with good friends all gathered together in the same room, online multiplayer will also now be an option.

Heave Ho 2 does the sequel thing of taking the original and adding to it. Underneath it all, it’s the same physics-puzzler shenanigans, but mixes things up with new level themes and puzzle styles.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/20/heave-ho-2-preview-hold-my-hand-and-help-me-make-a-chicken-burger/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: We Might Have Just Got Our Best Look At Link In The Legend Of Zelda Movie
item.content:encoded:
But how accurate is it..?

What do yWith principal photography now wrapped on The Legend of Zelda movie, it's likely that Nintendo and Columbia Pictures will want to start the hype train engine ahead of its release on 7th May 2027.

The movie's Director of Photography, Gyula Pados, may have jumped the gun slightly, though. In a now-deleted post on Instagram (thanks, Wario64), he showcased a clipboard to presumably commemorate the completion of principal photography.

The key image on the clipboard depicts Link standing next to the Triforce. Meanwhile, the word 'Umami' is displayed prominently on the right, with 'Director - Wes Ball' and 'Camera - Gyula Pados' just underneath.

So, is this our best look at Link so far? Well, possibly. One thing that immediately stands out is the fact that the character in the image looks nothing like Benjamin Evan Ainsworth, the 17-year-old actor cast to play the lead role.

So it seems likely, then, that this is merely some concept art. Even if that's the case, it's clearly an incredibly faithful depiction of Link that seems to be leaning into the aesthetics from Twilight Princess. Y'know, if you ignore the giant cape he seems to be rocking.

So we'll have to wait and see just how accurate this image really is. There's no denying its authenticity given it came directly from Gyula Pados, but whether the final portrayal of Link comes even close to the image remains to be seen.

What do you make of this image from Gyula Pados? Reckon it's our best look at Link so far? Let us know with a comment.

[source bsky.app]

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/we-might-have-just-got-our-best-look-at-link-in-the-legend-of-zelda-movie
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Piratical survival game Windrose sails past 1m copies sold, as player counts continue to rise
item.content:encoded:
Windrose - the piratical survival game from Kraken Express - has shifted 1m copies in just six days.

The developer shared the latest milestone with a celebratory post, acknowledging the "crew of fine brave captains sailing the seas of Windrose" since the game's widely-praised early access release last week. It added that not only has the game moved 1m copies, but Windrose also welcomed 200,000 concurrent players.

"Your support for the game is absolutely astonishing, and your patience while we are working on fixes is truly heart-warming. While we keep working on the game, we read your reviews and comments, watch your videos and streams, and sometimes, honestly, shed a pirate-y tear of happiness, because now when the game is out you share our dream of swashbuckling adventure," Kraken Express wrote in a blog post on Steam. "And there are boars, too, we know, but whatever does not send you flying into the nearest tree, makes you stronger, right?

"Thank you for this, captains. You are our inspiration in this journey, and YARRRRR, that’s going to be an exciting one."

It shared an accompanying picture of a boar for good measure (see below).

Windrose is set deep in the Caribbean, during an alternative version of the 18th century. You start off the game as a shipwrecked castaway, and need to get foraging and crafting to survive in the hostile world. Soon, though, you will be able to escape the island you have found yourself on, and take to the seas as a pirate. The game was originally envisaged as a free-to-play MMO, but player feedback during alpha testing convinced the team to pivot toward a more focused premium title, designed for one to four players. And, it seems like this decision has paid off for all involved.

Our Matt took the earlier Windrose demo out for a whirl, and he had a ship shape time with it. "Windrose already has my piratical heart aflame," he wrote in Eurogamer's Windrose feature. "I went from optimistic to completely sold."

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/windrose-1m-copies-celebration
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: CAPCOM’s PRAGMATA Sells More Than 1 Million Units in 48 Hours
item.content:encoded:
This morning, Japanese developer and publisher CAPCOM announced that its brand-new sci-fi intellectual property, PRAGMATA, is off to a great start, having sold more than 1 million units in its first 2 days on the market.

In the press release, CAPCOM noted that the IP (which has been in development for a long time) was crafted by a team of younger developers who successfully created an innovative gameplay experience by fusing action gameplay with puzzle elements (the real-time hacking mini-game). To offset the lack of a pre-existing fanbase or brand recognition, CAPCOM implemented a series of marketing initiatives that paid off, including the release of the PRAGMATA Sketchbook demo on all platforms. Moreover, they added support for the Nintendo Switch 2 console early to broaden the title's availability, which certainly helped them achieve this sales milestone.

The team shared this comment:

As a completely new IP, PRAGMATA represents a new challenge for Capcom, built from the ground up with an original world and gameplay concept. We are truly delighted that so many players around the world have enjoyed the game, enabling us to reach this milestone of one million units sold. Moving forward, we will continue making every effort to deliver the appeal of PRAGMATA to an even broader audience.

CAPCOM thus continues its streak of successful game releases, dating back all the way to 2017's Resident Evil 7: Biohazard. Only very few of their launches have been unsuccessful, namely the multiplayer third-person shooter Exoprimal and the action-strategy game Kunitsu-Gami: Path of the Goddess. To a lesser extent, even Dragon's Dogma 2 can be considered slightly less successful than most of the recent CAPCOM games. While it has sold over 4 million units to date, fans weren't so thrilled with it, and recent Steam reviews are now 'Mixed'. CAPCOM might be about to announce an expansion for it, though.

For more information about PRAGMATA, including links to our review and guides/walkthroughs, check out our dedicated hub page.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/capcom-pragmata-1-million-copies-48-hours/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nioh 3 update launches April 27, adds multiple high-difficulty Battle Scrolls
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5

Team NINJA hard at work on future updates and downloadable content.

Publisher Koei Tecmo and developer Team NINJA will release a free update for Nioh 3 on April 27, the companies announced. It will add multiple high-difficulty Battle Scrolls, new skills as rewards for completing those missions, and more.

Here is a message from Nioh 3 producer Kohei Shibata on the update:

We would first like to express our gratitude to the many players around the world who have taken the time to play Nioh 3 since its release. We are currently hard at work developing future updates and downloadable content for the game.

I would also like to take this opportunity to briefly introduce the free update scheduled for release on April 27. This update will primarily introduce new features and content designed to enhance the Nioh 3 gameplay experience.

Content included in the impending update:

  • Addition of multiple high-difficulty Battle Scrolls (side missions).
  • Addition of the Stone of Penance, which increases accessory +Values and rarities in exchange for higher difficulties.
  • Addition of new skills as rewards for completing selected high-difficulty Battle Scroll missions.
  • Balance adjustments to enhance certain Graces.

We are currently preparing for the release of this update, and we appreciate your patience until it becomes available.

Further details on each element will be shared via our official social media channels, so we encourage you to follow them for the latest information.

We will continue to provide ongoing updates for Nioh 3, including downloadable content releases planned for later this year.

In the meantime, we hope you will continue to enjoy playing Nioh 3.

Nioh 3 is available now for PlayStation 5 and PC via Steam.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/nioh-3-update-launches-april-27-adds-multiple-high-difficulty-battle-scrolls
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: This Week’s Japanese Game Releases: Kingdom’s Return: Time-Eating Fruit and the Ancient Monster, PRAGMATA for Switch 2, more
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS4
  • PS5
  • Smartphone
  • Switch
  • Switch 2
  • Xbox One
  • Xbox Series

Kiln, Dragon Quest Smash/Grow, Tides of Tomorrow, etc.

Kingdom’s Return: Time-Eating Fruit and the Ancient Monster for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, Switch, and PC, and PRAGMATA for Switch 2 are the highlights of this week’s Japanese video game releases.

Also due out this week are Kiln for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC, Dragon Quest Smash/Grow for iOS and Android, and Tides of Tomorrow for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC.

Get the full list of this week’s Japanese game releases below. It should be noted that Gematsu will receive a small percentage of money from purchases made through Play-Asia links. You can also save five percent with our multi-use “GEMATSU2025” coupon code.

Physical and Digital

  • Elminage ORIGINAL: Priestess of Darkness and The Ring of the Gods (Switch)
  • FRONT MISSION 3: Remake (PS5, PS4) – Already available digitally worldwide.
  • GIGASWORD (PS5, Switch) – Already available digitally worldwide.
  • Kingdom’s Return: Time-Eating Fruit and the Ancient Monster (PS5, Switch) – Launching digitally worldwide.
    • Limited Edition (PS5, Switch)
    • Asian Version
      • Standard Edition (PS5, Switch)
      • Limited Edition (PS5, Switch)
  • Matsurika no Kei -kEi- Tenmei kashokuden (Switch)
    • Special Edition (Switch)
    • Otomate Sweet Box (Switch)
  • Monochrome Mobius: Rights and Wrongs Forgotten New Price Edition (PS5, PS4)
  • PRAGMATA (Switch 2) – Already available worldwide.
    • Deluxe Edition (Switch 2)
    • Asian Version (Switch 2)
  • SHUTEN ORDER – Nintendo Switch 2 Edition (Switch 2) – Already available digitally. Physical edition launching worldwide.
    • North American Version (Switch 2, Switch)
    • European Version (Switch 2)
    • Asian Version (Switch 2)

Digital-Only

  • Albion Online (Xbox Series) – Launching worldwide.
  • ARTDINK GAME LOG: Aquanaut’s Holiday (Switch, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Combat Kart (PS5, Xbox Series, Switch) – Launching worldwide.
  • Causal Loop (PS5, Xbox Series, PC [Steam, Epic Games Store, GOG]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Dark Hours (PS5, Xbox Series) – Launching worldwide.
  • Dialoop (PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • DIMENSIONS 2 (Xbox Series, PC [Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Dragon is Dead (PS5, Xbox Series, Switch) – Launching worldwide.
  • Dragon Quest Smash/Grow (iOS, Android) – Launching worldwide.
  • Drop Duchy: Complete Edition (PS5, Switch) – Launching worldwide.
  • Farm Manager World (Xbox Series) – Launching worldwide.
  • Fruit Mountain Party (Switch 2, Switch, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • GluMe (PS5, Xbox Series, PS4, Xbox One, Switch) – Launching worldwide.
  • Jay and Silent Bob: Chronic Blunt Punch (PS5, Xbox Series, PS4, Switch, PC [Steam, Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Kiln (PS5, Xbox Series, PC [Steam, Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Kingdom’s Return: Time-Eating Fruit and the Ancient Monster (Xbox Series, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Kristala (PS5, Xbox Series, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Kumitantei: Old-School Slaughter (PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Nagi no Koi (PS4, Switch) – Launching worldwide.
  • OBLITACRATER (PS5, Xbox Series, PC [Steam, Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Our Metal Bowl (Xbox Series, PC [Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Party Club (PS5, Xbox Series, Xbox One, Switch, PC [Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Puzzle Bobble Everybubble! (PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Rumbral (PS5, Xbox Series, Switch, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Saint Slayer: Spear of Sacrilege (PS5, Xbox Series, PS4, Xbox One, Switch, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Save My Scrap (PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Shy Cats Hidden Orchestra 2 (Xbox Series, Xbox One, PC [Steam, Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Star Fire: Eternal Cycle (PS5, Xbox Series, PC [Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Sudden Strike 5 (Xbox Series, PC [Steam, Epic Games Store]) – Launching worldwide.
    • PlayStation 5 version launches May 20 in Japan.
  • Tides of Tomorrow (PS5, Xbox Series, PC [Steam]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Until Then (Xbox Series) – Launching worldwide.
  • Urban Jungle (Xbox Series, PC [Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Vampire Crawlers: The Turbo Wildcard from Vampire Survivors (PS5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, Switch, PC [Steam, Microsoft Store]) – Launching worldwide.
  • Wild Arms 4 (PS5, PS4) – Launching worldwide.

The PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and Switch titles listed above can be purchased through Japanese PlayStation Network and Nintendo accounts using prepaid cards:

  • PlayStation Store cards (yen): 1,100 / 3,000 / 5,000 / 10,000
  • Nintendo eShop cards (yen): 1,000 / 1,500 / 3,000 / 5,000 / 9,000

Additionally, direct top-ups for gacha games are available here, including:

  • Blue Protocol: Star Resonance
  • Genshin Impact
  • Honkai: Star Rail
  • Love and Deepspace
  • Marvel Rivals
  • Where Winds Meet
  • Zenless Zone Zero

Demos, DLC, Updates, etc.

  • Age of Mythology: Retold Downloadable Content “Obsidian Mirror” (PS5, Xbox Series, Xbox One, PC)
  • Dragon Ball FighterZ Downloadable Content Character Goku (Super Saiyan 4, DAIMA) (PS5, Xbox Series, PS4, Xbox One, Switch, PC)
  • Honkai: Star Rail Version 4.2 Update “So Laughed the Masses” (PS5, PC, iOS, Android)
  • Petit Planet “Stardrift Test” (PC, iOS, Android)


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/this-weeks-japanese-game-releases-kingdoms-return-time-eating-fruit-and-the-ancient-monster-pragmata-for-switch-2-more
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Goonya Monster spin-off games launch April 27 for Switch, PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Switch

All four titles currently in Early Access for PC.

MUTAN‘s Goonya Monster spin-off games—the hopping action game Toad’s Soul Hopper, runner game Pirarucu’s Money Rush, shooting game Snail’s Knock Out, and balance physics puzzle game Octo’s Balloon Challenge—will leave Early Access and launch for Switch and PC via Steam on April 27, the developer announced.

All four titles previously launched in Early Access on May 22, 2024.

Here is an overview of each game, via their store pages:

Toad’s Soul Hopper (Steam)

About

Toad’s Soul Hopper is a hopping action game where the dependable scientist Toad from Goonya Monster makes his mark.

His fellow Busters have been enlarged by the mad scientist, Anemone’s prank. To save them, Toad jumps into action with his custom-made hopping machine, the “Soul Hopper”!

…Jumping to save friends?

Key Features

  • Easy Controls? – Toad on his hopper is constantly bouncing. Press the button as he lands to charge power, then release it to make a big jump, adjusting the angle while charging.
  • Bouncing, Rebounding, Falling… – If Toad’s body or the side of the hopper hits a wall or the ground, he’ll rebound. One moment of carelessness can lead to a fall and a familiar view below.
  • A Treacherous Path – Platforms may collapse or catch fire, hindering your ascent.
  • Can You See the Summit? – Compete for the fastest time to the top with the local rankings, which store the top 10 scores. Aim higher than your past self!

Pirarucu’s Money Rush (Steam)

About

Pirarucu’s Money Rush is a running game featuring Pirarucu, a shopping-loving lady from Goonya Monster.

Pirarucu runs, leaps over cliffs, stomps on enemies, all amidst a rain of fireballs, just for the sake of shopping! Collect the coins scattered along the way and enjoy shopping at the checkpoints!

Key Features

  • Big Coins, Small Coins – There are both big and small coins scattered throughout the stages. Aim to grab the big coins as they offer bonus points!
  • Plan Your Shopping! – Bold shopping sprees might lead to running out of funds quickly. Plan your shopping strategically to ensure you can buy the necessary items!
  • Beware of Dangers Along the Way… – Small undead creatures and fireballs flow through the stages. Colliding with them will cause damage. Defeat the undead by stomping on them and gracefully dodge the fireballs by jumping!
  • Keep Running Towards the Next Shopping Trip – Shopping is a race against time. Pirarucu’s running speed increases steadily, so aim for the checkpoints while battling the thrill of the chase!

Snail’s Knock Out (Steam)

About

Snail’s Knock Out is a casual shooting game featuring Snail, who loves battles, from Goonya Monster.

After the chaos of Goonya Monster, Snail’s love for battle explodes in boredom!? Blast away the appearing undead to your heart’s content, shooting and dodging in stages that gradually speed up!

Key Features

  • Avoid What Falls and Hit the Undead to Blast Them Away! – Hitting rocks or undead reduces HP, so avoid rocks and aim to attack the undead!
  • Speed Up as You Progress! – As time passes, the stage speed increases along with Snail’s tension, so control Snail quickly!
  • Pick Up Weapons and Fight With Your Favorite Weapon! – Laser! Gatling! Arrow! Use the unique characteristics of weapons to blast away enemies! Defeating the undead that appear in the stage will drop souls. Collect souls to level up and strengthen your weapons!
  • Sweep Away With Bombs! Clear the Way! – When the bomb gauge is full, press the skill button to shoot bombs continuously and blast away all visible undead!
  • Local Rankings – Your score will be recorded in the top 10. Keep challenging yourself to surpass your past records!

Octo’s Balloon Challenge (Steam)

About

Octo’s Balloon Challenge is a balance physics puzzle game featuring Octo, the protagonist of Goonya Monster.

One day, Souls started falling from the sky for some reason? Octo and Goonyan took off into the sky with balloons to solve the mystery of the falling Souls!

Octo’s Balloon Challenge is a new sensation balance physics puzzle game. Control the tilt of the catching device with balloons and soar higher and higher!

Souls come in groups of 10 Souls combine to form a small box. Furthermore, combining three sets of 10 boxes makes them disappear, earning you a bonus score!

Master the balloons Decide the size and location to release the balloons and balance the catching device! If you rush and gather too much wind on one side, it will tilt, causing the Souls to fall.

Watch a new trailer below.

Release Date Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/goonya-monster-spin-off-games-launch-april-27-for-switch-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Battlefield 6 Fans Believe BF Studios Is Teasing a Las Vegas Map
item.content:encoded:
Battlefield 6 players think they’ve discovered that one of the three maps coming in Season 5 is set in Las Vegas after Battlefield Studios posted an enigmatic teaser last week.

The team at EA outlined its plans to keep players coming back throughout 2026 last week after it pulled back the curtain to reveal a host of changes aimed at directly addressing fan feedback. While much larger multiplayer maps and a server browser are just the tip of the iceberg of what was officially revealed, some players have already set their sights on a single image that teased one of the maps launching toward the end of the year.

"Today, I can also confirm that, as a holiday surprise, we’ll be dropping a third map into Season 5," Motive Studio executive producer Philippe Ducharme said in a video for the April 16 reveal while showing the first-look. "You’ll have to wait to learn about them, but I will say that the roadmap does offer a hint of where you’ll be fighting."

The image shows what appears to be an asphalt road with a colorful puddle surrounded by loose bullets. Knee-jerk reaction in the community believed that the rainbow effect might be caused by a mixture of gasoline, but others have pointed out the shape in the middle of the water matches up with the iconic Welcome to Fabulous Las Vegas sign in Las Vegas, Nevada.

It’s hard to make out on its own, but side by side, it’s easy to see why players have come to this conclusion. The long shape of the sign mostly lines up with the shape in the puddle, and the yellow-and-white lights seem to match, too. Others have pointed out that Las Vegas isn’t exactly known for being a rainy city, but there’s always the possibility the liquid we’re seeing could have come from another source.

One player who says they live in the casino town pointed out that the orange beams on the left side of the screen may actually be a reflection of the Mandalay Bay resort and casino. The same poster notes that the right side of the image could be showing a reflection of the green neon of the MGM Grand.

BF Studios gave players very little to chew on, but many have settled on the idea that Las Vegas will serve as the location of one of the upcoming Season 5 maps for Battlefield 6. Others aren’t so convinced, with other popular theories revolving around a return for the Siege of Shanghai and Dawnbreaker maps from Battlefield 4, or even a new map set in New York City.

With Season 3 set to add Railway to Golmud and Cairo Bazaar in May and Season 4 set to follow with Wake Island and Tsuru Reef in July, it will likely be some time before we see what Season 5 has in store for the fall. Although the late 2026 content update will break tradition by adding three maps when it launches for Battlefield 6, BF Studios warned that players shouldn’t expect three maps per season going forward.

"We still plan to stay with the current strategy we have, which is adapting our season and our seasonal structure to meet the experiences that we want our players to play," Ripple Effect executive producer Ryan McArthur said during a Q&A session that IGN attended last week.

Battlefield 6 launched for PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X | S October 10, 2025. As players continue to put the pieces together while they wait for future seasons, you can check out our 8/10 multiplayer review. You can also check out what studio design director Justin Wiebe had to say regarding the team’s approach to community feedback.

Photo by Clive Mason/Getty Images.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).



https://www.ign.com/articles/battlefield-6-fans-believe-bf-studios-is-teasing-a-las-vegas-map
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Windrose Early Access sales top one million in six days
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Cooperative pirate survival game a swashbuckling success.

Total sales for cooperative pirate survival game Windrose surpassed one million units in six days, developer Kraken Express (formerly Windrose Crew) announced.

“Ahoy, captains! We have just plundered another milestone—one million copies, and ‘we’ means ‘you’—a crew of fine brave captains sailing the seas of Windrose,” reads a message from the development team. “Moreover, today Windrose has assembled over 200,000 concurrent players! Your support for the game is absolutely astonishing, and your patience while we are working on fixes is truly heart-warming.”

The developer continued, “While we keep working on the game, we read your reviews and comments, watch your videos and streams, and sometimes, honestly, shed a pirate-y tear of happiness, because now when the game is out you share our dream of swashbuckling adventure. And there are boars, too, we know, but whatever does not send you flying into the nearest tree, makes you stronger, right?”

Windrose launched in Early Access for PC via Steam on April 14.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/windrose-early-access-sales-top-one-million-in-six-days
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon Ball FighterZ DLC character Goku (Super Saiyan 4, DAIMA) launches April 22
item.content:encoded:
Dragon Ball FighterZ downloadable content character Goku (Super Saiyan 4, DAIMA) will launch on April 22, publisher Bandai Namco and developer Arc System Works announced.

Goku (Super Saiyan 4, DAIMA) will be available via the “DAIMA Pack,” which includes the new character, 12 default colors, three Z stamps, and three lobby avatars.

Dragon Ball FighterZ is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, Switch, and PC via Steam.

Watch a new trailer below.

DLC Character Goku (Super Saiyan 4, DAIMA) Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/dragon-ball-fighterz-dlc-character-goku-super-saiyan-4-daima-launches-april-22
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon Ball: Sparking! ZERO DLC ‘Super Limit-Breaking NEO’ announced
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Bandai Namco and developer Spike Chunsoft have announced Dragon Ball: Sparking! ZERO downloadable content “Super Limit-Breaking NEO,” which adds over 30 new characters, more than 20 new customization options, new stages, and a new solo mode. It will launch this summer alongside a free update introducing a new battle system.

Here is an overview of the content, via Bandai Namco:

About

“Super Limit Breaking NEO” is a major expansion arriving in Summer 2026, designed to answer community expectations and elevate the experience to new heights.

With massive content additions across all key pillars, NEO represents the most ambitious evolution of the game to date.

What’s Bew in this Downloadable Content

  • Over 30 New Playable Characters – Over 30 additional fighters join the roster, including Super 17, Bardock (Super Saiyan), Champa, and Vegeta (GT)—with several characters making their Budokai Tenkaichi series debut for the first time.
    • Grandpa Gohan, Mercenary Tao, Demon King Piccolo, Bardock Super Saiyan, Zangya, Pikkon, Hell Fighter 17, Super 17, Champa, Nam, Eighter, General Blue, Tora, Fasha, Chilled, Salza, Mighty Mask, Supreme Kai, Uub (Kid), King Vegeta, Cheelai, Jaco, Vegeta (GT), Vegeta (GT) Super Saiyan, Trunks (GT), Trunks (GT) Super Saiyan, Nuova Shenron (GT)…
  • Expanded Customization – More than 20 new customization options, including costumes and skills. Recreate iconic Dragon Ball scenes or shape your own battle style by equipping favorite super and ultimate techniques for even more intense and personal combat.
  • New Stages – Four new stages expand the battlefield, including Kami’s Palace, a legendary location for training and combat, and Stratosphere (Planet Vegeta), where Bardock clashed with Frieza’s forces—enhancing imaginative and cinematic battles.
  • New Solo Mode: Limit Breaker Journey – A brand-new single-player experience dedicated to deepening players’ connection with their favorite characters. Progress through branching scenarios, fights, and events, train characters through battle, earn unique rewards, and carry your growth into online play.

Dragon Ball: Sparking! ZERO is available now for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Switch 2, Switch, and PC via Steam.

Watch a new trailer below.

DLC “Super Limit-Breaking NEO” Trailer

English

Japanese



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/dragon-ball-sparking-zero-dlc-super-limit-breaking-neo-announced
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 announced for PS5, Xbox Series, and PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Xbox Series

Dragon Ball New Game Project AGE 1000 revealed.

Publisher Bandai Namco and developer Dimps have officially announced Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3, formerly Dragon Ball New Game Project AGE 1000, for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, and PC (Steam). It will launch in 2027.

Here is an overview of the game, via Bandai Namco:

About

Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 is a completely new Dragon Ball experience, where you decide the hero you want to be in an unexplored Dragon Ball world.

AGE 1000: A Whole New World

Step into a brand-new future Dragon Ball universe filled with unique, original characters brought to life by Akira Toriyama, the original author of Dragon Ball. In AGE 1000, journey through developed and vibrant West City. Explore the bustling city, encounter characters, and uncover exciting new stories.

Take your place in the story and experience what awaits in West City. Guided into the ranks of the Great Saiyan Squad, you’ll fight alongside allies as events there begin to take shape.

In Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3, the world of Dragon Ball continues to expand.

More information coming soon!

Watch a new trailer below. View the screenshots at the gallery.

Announce Trailer

English

Japanese

Screenshots

  • ❮
  • ❯


https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/dragon-ball-xenoverse-3-announced-for-ps5-xbox-series-and-pc
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Bandai Namco Officially Reveals What's Next For The Dragon Ball Xenoverse Series
item.content:encoded:
Including DLC and a brand new game

At Dragon Ball Games Battle Hour 2026 this week, Bandai Namco shared two announcements tied to the future of its Dragon Ball Xenoverse series.

Firstly, "the last DLC" for Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 - officially titled Future Saga Chapter 4 - will launch in Summer 2026.

And after rumours and even a title leak in February, Bandai Namco has officially confirmed Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3. It will launch in 2027 and is currently confirmed for Steam, PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X|S.

There's no mention of a Nintendo release (at least right now), but Bandai Namco has already released DRAGON BALL: Sparking! ZERO on the Switch 2, and Digimon Story Time Stranger is coming to Nintendo's new hybrid platform in July.

Xenoverse 3's official reveal follows a teaser of the new game earlier this year, when it was known as "Age 1000".

At the same Dragon Ball Games Battle Hour event this week, Bandai Namco revealed the new DLC character for Dragon Ball FighterZ will be arriving next week. It also gave Dragon Ball: Sparking! ZERO players a look at the upcoming Super Limit-Breaking Neo DLC.

What do you think of these Dragon Ball Xenoverse announcements? Would you be interested in playing Xenoverse 3 on Nintendo's platform? Will you be checking out the last update for Xenoverse 2? Let us know in the comments.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/bandai-namco-officially-reveals-whats-next-for-the-dragon-ball-xenoverse-series
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: THQ Nordic Teases 7 Unannounced Games for Nintendo Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
THQ Nordic has updated its website, revealing that it is working on several games for the Nintendo Switch family of consoles. Specifically, 7 unannounced projects are coming for the new Nintendo Switch 2.

The original Nintendo Switch also lists six unannounced projects, and the seventh is The Eternal Life of Goldman, a game revealed during the recent Xbox Partner Showcase. It is not known whether that is one of the games coming to the Switch 2 in its own dedicated edition. However, all regular Nintendo Switch games also work on the successor platform.

THQ Nordic is Hard at Work for the Nintendo Switch Consoles

THQ Nordic, which is, of course, a revival of the former THQ brand, is working very hard to release games for the Nintendo Switch family of consoles. In addition to these unannounced titles, the company just revealed that it was bringing several new games to both the original Nintendo Switch and its successor. Among those games are Disney’s Epic Mickey, both Destroy All Humans games, and SpongeBob SquarePants: Titans of the Tide. Learn more about those releases at this link.

While it’s currently unknown what’s in the works from THQ Nordic, plenty of Summer reveal events are on the horizon that could provide further details. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available. For now, check out more on The Eternal Life of Goldman at this link.

What are you hoping to see from THQ Nordic on the Nintendo Switch 2? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/thq-nordic-teases-7-unnanounced-games-for-nintendo-switch-2/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 Announced for PC and Consoles in First Trailer
item.content:encoded:
Bandai Namco has announced Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 for PC via Steam, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X | S.

The announcement arrived alongside the Dragon Ball Games Battle Hour 2026 event in Los Angeles today and comes with a first official trailer for the anime action-RPG follow-up. It’s news that came as a shock to fans and arrived as official confirmation that a follow-up is finally on the way after Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 launched in 2016, almost 10 years ago.

Doubling the surprise is the fact that Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 was technically already revealed by Bandai Namco earlier this year as what was previously called Age 1000. That title now serves as the name of the new world in which the game is set, with the trailer showing off some of what it has to offer.

"As revealed in the announcement trailer for DRAGON BALL XENOVERSE 3, players will get to explore and uncover exciting new stories in West City, the vibrant new world set in AGE 1000," an official description said. "Guided into the ranks of the Great Saiyan Squad, you’ll fight alongside allies as events there begin to take shape.

"The game combines an epic story-driven RPG experience that will take DRAGON BALL fans on an unforgettable, action-packed journey into this new universe and its cast of characters."

Bandai Namco says there will be more to share about Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 "in the months to come." While we wait to see how it will expand on the work of the late Akira Toriyama, you can check out our 7.5/10 review of Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2. At the time, we called it "an ambitious, if rough-edged experience, with deceptively deep RPG and brawling elements."

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).



https://www.ign.com/articles/dragon-ball-xenoverse-3-announced-for-pc-and-consoles-in-first-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Best Deals Today: Apple AirTag 4-Pack, 3 for $33 Movie Deal, Sony WH-1000XM5 Headphones, and More
item.content:encoded:
A new weekend has arrived, and today, you can save big on Apple AirTags, 4K Movies, AirPods Pro 3, Super Mario Galaxy + Super Mario Galaxy 2, the limited edition Marathon DualSense, and more. Check out our top picks for Sunday, April 19, below.

3 4K Movies for $33

By far, my favorite deal this weekend is the Amazon 3 for $33 sale, where you can score 3 4K films for $33. This is one of the best deals on 4K movies in 2026 so far, especially considering each film ends up at $11 a piece. There are some excellent options available, including Jurassic Park, Tenet, Blade Runner: The Final Cut, and more.

Refurbished Sony 4K OLED BRAVIA 8 TV for $1049.99

Sony OLED 4K Ultra HD TV BRAVIA 8 Smart Google TV (2024) (Refurbished)

Woot has a great deal on this Sony 4K OLED BRAVIA 8 TV, with refurbished units available for $1049.99. Sony's Bravia line is well respected and usually more expensive, making this TV well worth a look if you're in the market for a new one. If you're aiming for the best picture quality and rich colors, you cannot do better than OLED, and these units are often still like new.

Apple AirPods Pro 3 for $199.99

Apple AirPods Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds

AirPods Pro 3 are on sale today for $199.99 at Amazon. As the third-generation model, these earbuds are equipped with new features like a built-in heartbeat sensor, newly designed eartips for maximum noise cancellation, and much more. Grab a pair today before the sale ends.

Resident Evil Generation Pack Still Available

Resident Evil Generation Pack - Nintendo Switch 2

The Resident Evil Generation Pack is still available at Amazon for $89.99, and this is one deal you do not want to miss. This collection packs in Resident Evil VII, Resident Evil Village, and Resident Evil Requiem all for $89.99, which means you are essentially paying $10 for both VII and Village. Capcom has removed this deal as of March 31 digitally, and it's only a matter of time before it's gone physically, too.

Marathon DualSense for $69.68

PlayStation DualSense® Wireless Controller – Marathon Limited Edition

You can score the limited edition Marathon DualSense controller for $69.68 on Amazon today. This controller features unique designs inspired by the game and its iconography. Normally priced at $84.99, this deal saves you 18%, which is perfect for anyone in need of a new controller.

Super Mario Galaxy + Super Mario Galaxy 2 for $54.99

Super Mario Galaxy + Super Mario Galaxy 2 - Nintendo Switch

Super Mario Galaxy + Super Mario Galaxy 2 released for Nintendo Switch last Fall, and today, you can score this double pack for $54.99 at Woot! These two adventures are some of Mario's greatest, making this a must-own game for any Switch owner. Plus, there's a free update for Nintendo Switch 2 that enables 4K support.

Nintendo Switch 2 - Super Mario Galaxy 1+2 Bundle

Nintendo Switch 2 - Super Mario Galaxy 1+2 Bundle

The limited time Nintendo Switch 2 - Super Mario Galaxy 1+2 Bundle is live now, and you can save $20 off a copy of Super Mario Galaxy 1+2 for a limited time. If you've yet to pick up a Switch 2 console, now is a great time, especially with Yoshi and the Mysterious Book set to launch next month.

Apple AirTag 4-Pack for $56.99

(4 PACK) Apple AirTags (1st Gen)

Apple AirTags are one of the best purchases you can make as an iPhone user. After pairing it once, you can instantly throw an AirTag almost anywhere, including a wallet, purse, backpack, suitcase, or even a Nintendo Switch 2 case. You can save over $40 today at Woot on a 4-pack.

Sony WH-1000XM5 Headphones for $249.99

Sony WH-1000XM5 Premium Noise Canceling Headphones

Amazon has the incredible Sony WH-1000XM5 headphones on sale today for $249.99. In our 9/10 review, we wrote, "The Sony WH-1000XM5 is hands down the best sounding and most impressive noise-canceling headphones around. They demand a high premium but the cost of audio excellence is well worth it with this pair of headphones."

Star Wars Outlaws for $19.99

Star Wars Outlaws - Limited Edition, PlayStation 5

Star Wars Outlaws is on sale for $19.99 today, marking a new low price for this one. The open world game stars Kay Vess, a thief who explores the criminal underworld in an era set between The Empire Strikes Back and Return of the Jedi. If you're on the hunt for a new Star Wars adventure, look no further than Outlaws.

Noah Hunter is a freelance writer and reviewer with a passion for games and technology. He co-founded Final Weapon, an outlet focused on nonsense-free Japanese gaming (in 2019) and has contributed to various publishers writing about the medium.



https://www.ign.com/articles/best-deals-for-april-19-2026
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Slay the Spire 2 Roadmap Prioritizes Healthy Workflow Over Dates
item.content:encoded:
Slay the Spire 2 is trying to stay ahead of unkept promises by issuing a roadmap without firm delivery dates.

Slay the Spire 2 has had strong success since its debut in Steam Early Access in March 2025. It still sees a high player count with nearly 300,000 concurrent players in the last 24 hours at the time of this writing. Within one month, the game even sold 5 million copies.

Mega Crit’s Slay the Spire 2 Roadmap

Released as part of the April 2026 Newosletter from Mega Crit, the roadmap promises new content, including two alternate acts, a new character, and more cards. Some of the goals for the far future include “true victory.” These are mentioned alongside ongoing updates to quality-of-life features and bug fixes.

The full roadmap can be seen below:

Features and Systems:
– Steam Workshop support
– Support for more languages
– The Bestiary, a way to view and learn about the enemies in the game
– Experimental game modes

Ongoing Tasks:
– Fixings bugs
– Compatibility and performance improvements
– Game balance and tweaks
– Quality of life features
– Audio polish (more silly voices)
– Visual polish (less silly placeholder art)

Content (No spoilers, it’s for your own good!)
– Alternate Act 2
– New Character
– Alternate Act 3
– More cards, events, relics, and potions

Further off in the Future
– Ports to other consoles/platforms/mobile/etc
– Steam Achievements & Tradings Cards
– “True Victory” + Everything that comes with it

No Firm Release Dates for These Slay the Spire 2 Changes

Casey from Mega Crit also addressed the lack of release dates. In their statement, they discuss being a small development team and choosing to work at a healthy pace rather than overly promising a firm timeline.

“We evaluate our tasks each week and work on what feels most impactful. It’s not the most organized method, but this allows spontaneous experimentation like the dialogues you have with the Ancients or the existence of a Room Full of Cheese. We won’t massively expand the size of the studio to finish the game faster. Exacting deadlines produce sloppy uninspired work and I don’t want Sloppy Spire 2, I want Slay the Spire 2,” the statement reads.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor this game’s development and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Have you checked out Mega Crit’s Slay the Spire 2 yet? If so, what do you think? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/slay-the-spire-2-roadmap-prioritizes-healthy-workflow-over-dates/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 Coming in 2027
item.content:encoded:
Bandai Namco has officially announced Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3. First revealed as Age 1000 in January. Many players initially expected the game to be a follow-up to Dragon Ball Online, a 2010 MMORPG, but it has now been confirmed to be the next Xenoverse title.

Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 was initially leaked in February after a social media blunder on Bandai Namco’s Southeast Asian YouTube page. Now, however, Bandai has revealed the full trailer and announced that the game is coming in 2027.

Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 Trailer Reveal

The first trailer for the next Xenoverse game was nearly 4 minutes long and featured English voice acting for Bulma, as well as several stand-ins for custom character creations. Of course, the Xenoverse line is all about players creating their own characters and inserting them into the Dragon Ball Z universe.

The Game Features Original Work From Akira Toriyama

Despite his unfortunate passing in 2024, Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 will feature work from the creator of the Dragon Ball series, Akira Toriyama.

It had previously been stated that work on the project began in the late 2010s and that more than half a decade had been invested in the game, including never-before-seen original characters from Toriyama and key world-building elements. His name is even featured early in the trailer.

Although Bulma is the only mainstay Dragon Ball character featured in the trailer, Piccolo is also teased for a second. In one shot, around the 30-second mark, you can see what looks to be Piccolo’s cape from the back.

It will have been a full decade since the release of Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 by the time this new game releases. The last title was released in October 2016 and has received several quality-of-life updates to ensure its longevity.

Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 is set for release on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam in 2027. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

For more Dragon Ball on Insider Gaming, check out the upcoming DLC for Dragon Ball Sparking Zero.

What are you hoping to see from Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/dragon-ball-xenoverse-3-coming-in-2027/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Modder Discovers Abandoned Dark Souls II Level: Even More Sewers
item.content:encoded:
The Dark Souls series has hosted no shortage of damp, dank, poisonous places. According to modder and YouTuber Doneda, we only saw the tip of the iceberg. In a new video, we’re given a tour of what was originally imagined for Dark Souls 2’s “The Gutter.”

Dark Souls 2’s development was infamously rocky. With Hidetaka Miyazaki busy on Bloodborne, Dark Souls II was treated to a carousel of directors as the studio kept finding itself unsatisfied. Not only did this give us what’s often considered the runt of the series, but troves of cut content. Even if it didn’t make it to the final game, uncovering this abandoned material only takes a bit of digging. Before long, you’re deep underground.

“If you know very little about the subject of Souls modding, every Dark Souls game has something called Map Studio Binary,” says Doneda in a new video. “The map studio binary is what actually tells the game what to load and what not to load in that map… When they cut content from the game, especially when it’s about map, geometry, collision, and so on, they don’t actually delete these assets. They just remove them from the map studio binary. So, the engine of the game does not know that they should be loaded in there.”

Not just discovered, but restored, appears to be the original vision for the Gutter. Reminiscent of Blighttown, the Gutter was cavernous and ramshackle. Impaired vision and perilous drops making it one of the more contentious Dark Souls locations. It appears that at one point the Gutter was far more ambitious. Instead of mere twig scaffolding, there was once an entire civilization. Even devoid of enemies and items, the vast pillars of megastructures and ornate passages showcase what could have been.

In the nearly hour-long tour of this area, Doneda comes across notes in kanji, boss arenas and even a tavern. The allure of what’s available makes Doneda feel as if the developers hoped someone would one day come across this space.



https://kotaku.com/modder-discovers-abandoned-dark-souls-ii-level-even-more-sewers-2000688887
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Casey Affleck-Led Bitcoin Thriller Made By AI Seems Desperate For Some Sucker Studio To Buy It
item.content:encoded:
It’s clear that Hollywood studios want to use AI in movie making. How to do so remains elusive, as the tech’s shortcomings become immediately obvious in practice and prohibitively expensive even for devout firms. Still, even a half-baked dynamic could fetch a decent price tag. The Bourne Identity and Jumper director Doug Liman is taking his AI-generated film to this year’s Cannes, looking for a buyer. And as long as we’re being painfully on the nose, the movie is about bitcoin.

The Wrap’s Emily Zemler visited the set of Bitcoin: Killing Satoshi. The thriller follows model turned reporter (Gal Gadot) being recruited by a crypto millionaire (Pete Davidson) to investigate a doctor (Casey Affleck) who may be Satoshi Nakamoto, the alias of bitcoin’s elusive creator. Filmed out of a car showroom, the actors and outfits are real, but all the lighting, sets and post-production will be assigned to generative AI.

“We decided to use AI very early on,” Ryan Kavanaugh tells Zemler. “We budgeted out what it would be to do it practically and it was over $300 million. It has about 200 distinct locations, from Antarctica to Antigua to Vegas, which is obviously unproducible. We realized we could bring down the cost by utilizing some of the AI tools out there.”

Clearly seeking a middle ground with skeptics and labor groups, Bitcoin still employed 200 crew, half of which are cast members. Chroma key productions have been the norm for a while, the novelty here being that the task of replacing green screens with the final product will be handled by 55 “AI artists.” Producer Lawrence Grey tells The Wrap that they’re using AI “the right way.”

Even if it impresses the board, AI cheerleaders are constantly finding out that the public do not view the technology favorably, nor looking to pay ticket price for it. Disney recently laid off 1,000 staff at Marvel Studios, hitting their effects team hardest. It forecasts where studios would like production trends to bend.

The current play is marketing your use of AI to cut a deal with anyone willing to invest in it, regardless if the tech will every pay out. This happens to be what Casey Affleck’s brother Ben did with Netflix just last month for the sum of $600 million. All the funnier to pin this project on a thriller on bitcoin, another tax on the gullible, about the identity of Nakamoto, who The New York Times recently revealed may be British computer scientist Adam Back.



https://kotaku.com/bitcoin-thriller-billing-itself-as-first-studio-quality-ai-generated-movie-pete-davidson-gal-gadot-2000688877
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: No Rest for the Wicked Devs Haven’t Forgotten Consoles
item.content:encoded:
Moon Studios reminds players that the console versions of No Rest for the Wicked are still in development as development progresses toward version 1.0.

No Rest for the Wicked is an action role-playing game that is currently in Early Access on Steam. Despite being in Early Access for 2 years, the game has managed to captivate players worldwide. Recently, the team celebrated selling 1.5 million copies. Even since that announcement in February, that number has moved up, and the team is now celebrating its two-year anniversary on the market.

No Rest for the Wicked Devs Say They Haven’t Forgotten Console Players

In a post celebrating 2 years since the game entered Early Access, the developers of No Rest for the Wicked stated on Steam that they are still hard at work on the game’s full launch. They also took the time to assure console players that they haven’t been forgotten.

Furthermore, the developers at Moon Studios stated that they wouldn’t be slowing down and they’re proud of the game that they’ve built. They also thanked players not only for supporting them but also for challenging them to do better.

“It’s been TWO YEARS since No Rest for the Wicked entered Early Access. Since then, this game has grown and evolved in ways we could only pull off because of you.

“1.7 million copies sold, and counting. You’ve supported us, challenged us, and pushed us to do better. That’s exactly how we like it. We’re hard at work toward 1.0. And console players, we haven’t forgotten you,” a portion of the message reads.

Moon Studios, which was known prior to the release of this ARPG for the Ori series, plans to raise the game’s price to $60 when the full version finally launches. Learn more about the reasoning behind that decision at this link.

Sound off on this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/no-rest-for-the-wicked-devs-havent-forgotten-consoles/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: DC Universe Online's Superman Day Event Is Live Through April 27
item.content:encoded:
DC Universe Online is marking Superman's first appearance in Action Comics with its annual Superman Day Event, running now through April 27. If you've been looking for a reason to log back in, Daybreak is handing you one with cosmetics drawn from two very different corners of the Superman mythos.

The event pulls its aesthetic from both Absolute Superman and Superman Beyond, and that split actually makes for an interesting reward lineup. On the unlimited side, you're looking at the Beyond Kryptonian Slimline Chest and Legs Styles alongside emblems from both the Absolute and Beyond lines. One-time account rewards include ten Flawless Nth Metal, 10,000 Ally Favor, 50 Stabilizer Fragments, and supply boxes for both Mogo Sprouts and Source Marks. Characters also get a one-time Skill Point Token, which is the kind of quiet, practical reward that tends to matter more than the cosmetics for players working through their builds.

Content-wise, the event funnels players into Doomed Metropolis for five open world daily missions, a set of weekly missions including bounties for both Heroes and Villains, and a slate of featured instances spanning solo, duo, alert, and raid formats. Last Son of Krypton, Earth 3: The Visitor, Phantom Zone, and the Doomsday raid are all on the table. This hits hot on the heels of the springtime event that released earlier this month, shoving a ton of content out in short order.

While DCUO has a few sales going on, you can pick up some freebies with code giveaways (such as the one above) throughout the week, so keep your eyes peeled on the game's socials. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/dc-universe-onlines-superman-day-event-is-live-through-april-27-2000137846
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Little Guy Solves Big Puzzles In Gecko Gods
item.content:encoded:
I usually have a low tolerance for “just a little guy” games. But the moment it’s about a bird or small reptile instead of a mouse or dog, I’m afraid you have me cornered. That’s just one of the reasons I’ve had my eye trained on Gecko Gods, a vibrantly colorful adventure about ancient temples, island obstacles, and a tiny teal lizard using a personal sailboat.

In Gecko Gods, you play as a small reptile washed ashore on a mysterious, serene island. Across this archipelago, you come across a vast network of abandoned temples. Within are statues and shrines seemingly built to deities that share your likeness, albeit 900 times larger than you. Overcoming your stature, you’ll push, pull, and prod your way around ancient mechanisms to unlock new chambers and strange magics. Gobble insects, battle sentries, and board your own boat to turn a big world larger, especially for a squat creature such as yourself.

Gorgeous pastel tones, breezy sounds and relaxed landscapes betray a game that can still lash its tongue. Even medium-sized threats can prove to be a difficult challenge at your height. The ability to make bold leaps and climb across ceilings invites you to think a little more three dimensionally about dungeon puzzles and exploration, at least compared to your usual Zelda fare.

For those who enjoy tomb raiding on their own terms, or open world adventure games pared down on quests and collectables, Gecko Gods is a charming afternoon expedition. Plus, us lizard-heads finally getting some more options out here. Watch in real time as my cute animal video gaming reservations melt away. And if you’d happen to prefer a gecko-starring game with wisenheimer jokes about Bruce Vilanch, well as it happens there are a surprising amount of video games for you too.

Gecko Gods is now available on Steam, PlayStation and Nintendo Switch.



https://kotaku.com/little-guy-solves-big-puzzles-in-gecko-gods-steam-indie-game-2000688872
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Windrose Sells 1 Million Copies And Hits New Player Milestone
item.content:encoded:
Windrose continues to find success, currently ranking fourth among Steam’s most-played games. The Kraken Express title was released on April 14. Since that time, they have amassed over 7,000 reviews with a very positive overall score.

The game is a pirate-themed survival, crafting, open-world PvE game, and it’s so strongly connected with players that it currently has 220,718 players at the time of this writing. The only three games with more concurrent players are Counter-Strike 2, Dota 2, and PUBG: Battlegrounds.

Kraken Express Expresses Gratitude for Windrose’s Success

In a Twitter post, the development team not only commented on the concurrent player milestone but also announced that the game had sold over 1 million copies. Additionally, they expressed their gratitude to the player base for not only supporting the game but also for their patience as they continue to work on fixes for the title.

“Ahoy, captains! We have just plundered another milestone – 1,000,000 copies, and “we” means “you” – a crew of fine brave captains sailing the seas of Windrose. Moreover, today Windrose has assembled over 200,000 concurrent players! Your support for the game is absolutely astonishing, and your patience while we are working on fixes is truly heart-warming. While we keep working on the game, we read your reviews and comments, watch your videos and streams, and sometimes, honestly, shed a pirate-y tear of happiness, because now when the game is out you share our dream of swashbuckling adventure. And there are boars, too, we know, but whatever does not send you flying into the nearest tree, makes you stronger, right?

“Thank you for this, captains. You are our inspiration in this journey, and YARRRRR, that’s going to be an exciting one,” the team stated in a separate message on Steam.

Windrose was originally announced at the PC Gaming Show: Most Wanted Showcase in December 2025 and had great success during a Steam Next Fest demo in February. Now, they just have to keep the sales afloat.

What do you think about the game so far if you played it? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/windrose-sells-1-million-copies-and-hits-new-player-milestone/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Windrose Celebrates 1 Million Copies Sold as Player Counts Climb
item.content:encoded:
Cooperative pirate survival game Windrose has reached 1 million copies sold less than a week after its Early Access launch for PC.

Pocketpair Publishing and Kraken Express announced the sales milestone with an update post on Steam today. It’s an announcement that saw it sail past the marker six days after launching for PC via the Valve platform and Epic Games Store April 14.

Reaching 1 million copies is a feat worth celebrating for Kraken as it charts a course through Early Access development, especially as its concurrent player count on Steam alone continues to climb. It’s managed to climb to 200,000 active pirates on Steam alone, with SteambDB recording a peak of 211,177 at the time of this piece’s publication.

Thank you! The boar sends its two-tusked regards.

Jokes aside, your support has been incredible, and your patience while we work on fixes means the world to us.

Moreover, Windrose surpassed 200,000 concurrent players on Steam!

Thank you kindly, on behalf of our entire studio. pic.twitter.com/axHyxc8wQU

— Windrose (@PlayWindrose) April 19, 2026

"Your support for the game is absolutely astonishing, and your patience while we are working on fixes is truly heart-warming," Kraken said. "While we keep working on the game, we read your reviews and comments, watch your videos and streams, and sometimes, honestly, shed a pirate-y tear of happiness, because now when the game is out you share our dream of swashbuckling adventure."

Windrose, previously known as Crosswind, debuted to ‘Very Positive’ reviews when it embarked on its Early Access journey last week. Kraken sells it as a "PvE survival adventure in the Age of Piracy," telling fans it will work with them through the development process in the hopes of reaching a 1.0 launch sometime in the next "1.5 to 2.5 years."

Players can head out on the sea alone or play with friends via co-op, with the developers estimating the final release will feature "around 50% more content." We said Windrose "made a strong first impression” and praised its “exploration and progression" in our review so far last week.

While Windrose charges ahead, you can see our 10 essential ship tips. You can also see our best suggestions for how to quickly farm bells.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/windrose-celebrates-1-million-copies-sold-as-player-counts-climb
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Celebrate MapleStory's 21st Anniversary With The Maple University Event
item.content:encoded:
The classic MMORPG MapleStory is celebrating its 21st anniversary with Maple University, a scholarly-themed event that has players taking on monsters and mini-games. 

Maple University offers two major tracks to follow. The Basic Lecture lets you complete Gen Ed, Majors and Internships to get Credits for Rewards. Meanwhile, the Special Lectures let players spend Mu Gong and Ephenia Points accumulated from the Basic Lecture for progression buffs. 

It’s not without perks, though, as the Rowdy Campus Life and Glorious Graduation skills are activated by a full gauge of killed monsters to give an EXP boost. Some buffs are also offered by the Hasty Hunting MAX event with its own missions to tackle, and further buffs are offered by Mano by exploring the university and picking up the Scholarship Kit.

A little Club Fair takes place during this event, with six mini-games offered to let players earn EXP Coupons and furniture. Players will be able to vote in the Monster Election, essentially a team progression event where players will complete activities including Club Fair mini-games to progress their candidates’ progress and gain unique perks per candidate. There’s also a customizable student ID for the event. 

Full details on everything available for MapleStory’s 21st anniversary event are available on the official web page for the event. 

In the meantime, Nexon has been running the Closed Beta Test for the Classic World edition of the game. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/celebrate-maplestorys-21st-anniversary-with-the-maple-university-event-2000137844
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
